title: Investigating a flood: guidance for lead local flood authorities
body: "This guidance provides a supporting framework for lead local flood authorities (LLFAs) to conduct flood investigations under section 19 of the Flood and Water Management Act 2010.  The guidance explains how LLFAs can:   decide whether to investigate a flood carry out a flood investigation analyse findings develop recommendations with partners create and publish a report   Principles of this guidance  Consistency  This guidance provides a supporting framework for all flood investigations, which will help provide more consistency at a regional and national level. It provides examples of good practice, tools and technology that can help with tasks and analysis.  Proportionate and local approach  This guidance provides flexibility for proportionate and scalable local approaches in all aspects of the flood investigation.  Partnership  This guidance helps LLFAs to communicate and work with flood partners and the community. It emphasises the importance of risk management authorities (RMAs) collaborating to:   share data and technology understand the causes of flooding make recommendations   Promoting action  This guidance aims to make sure that the recommendations from flood investigations help lead to positive outcomes for flooded communities.  "

taxons.title: River maintenance, flooding and coastal erosion
